title: Chain-reconciliation four-field freeze + D6 projection/loader counters
intent: For positions where corrected_executable_economics_eligible is True, chain reconciliation never overwrites entry_price, cost_basis_usd, size_usd, or shares; counters cost_basis_chain_mutation_blocked_total{field}, position_projection_field_dropped_total, and position_loader_field_defaulted_total are emitted in the same commit.
test_commands: python -m pytest -q -k 'chain_reconciliation or lifecycle_events or portfolio'

asserted_invariants.text: If position.corrected_executable_economics_eligible is True, chain_reconciliation.py's RESCUE branch (lines 531-577 region), SIZE-MISMATCH branch (lines 614-652 region), and QUARANTINE branch (lines 683-695 region) cannot mutate entry_price, cost_basis_usd, size_usd, or shares. Each blocked attempt increments cost_basis_chain_mutation_blocked_total{field=<one of the four>}.
If position.corrected_executable_economics_eligible is False (legacy default), every existing chain-fact mutation site behaves as it does today. No regression for legacy positions.

When src/state/portfolio.py serializes a Position with corrected_executable_economics_eligible=True and any of the four locked D6 fields is non-default-zero from chain context, position_projection_field_dropped_total{field=<one of the four>} increments and the projected row carries the FillAuthority value, not the chain-context value.

When _position_from_projection_row(...) reads a row whose locked D6 field is missing, position_loader_field_defaulted_total{field} increments and the in-memory Position uses the default; the row is not silently zero-defaulted.
T1B (chain-reconciliation freeze) and T1D (projection/loader counters) land in the same commit; the executor MUST NOT split this into separate B/D commits.

_planner_notes.f5_count: 20 distinct mutation lines across 5 branches; T1BD MUST guard every one or its invariant is partial.
_planner_notes.subtle_finding: Position has multiple share-flavored fields (shares, shares_filled, shares_remaining, shares_submitted, chain_shares). T1BD locks `shares` and `chain_shares` chain-mutation; shares_filled / shares_remaining are FillAuthority-derived and out of scope.
_planner_notes.topology_admission: BLOCKED at planner time — generic profile does not admit src/state/chain_reconciliation.py. Operator may need to add an 'r3 corrected economics chain freeze' digest profile before dispatch.
operator_only_blockers_at_planner_time: T0.2 RiskGuard still RUNNING (PID 14177); operator must unload before dispatch.
T0.3 venue quiescence not yet attested.
_planner_notes.k0_k1_classification: K0/K1 STATE TRUTH — lifecycle/chain reconciliation. Requires opus critic per ORCHESTRATOR_RUNBOOK §12 T1BD row. Paired counters land together (no split commits).
_planner_notes.closeout_drift_resolution: lifecycle_events.py was listed in files_touched but the executor never wrote to it — the file was inspection-only context for the chain-reconciliation 4-field freeze. Critic at T1BD review verified zero git-diff against lifecycle_events.py; the listing is an over-permissive scope artifact. Documented as read-only context, never mutated, rather than removing from files_touched to preserve the audit trail of which files the executor consulted.
